disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Central blurring of vision in the right eye developing over an hour or two, about 4 hours before contact
trigger_question_examples: What have you noticed with your vision?
Which eye is affected and how did it come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's my right eye — the middle of my vision went blurry over an hour or two this afternoon and it's still like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dull ache behind the right eye that is made worse by moving the eye
trigger_question_examples: Is there any pain in or around the eye?
Does anything make the eye feel worse — does moving it hurt?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, there's a dull ache behind that eye, and it gets worse when I move my eye around.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pins-and-needles sensation over the right cheek beginning at the same time as the visual change
trigger_question_examples: You mentioned your cheek — tell me about that feeling.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My right cheek feels like pins and needles — it started around the same time as the blurry vision.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No arm or leg weakness, no difficulty speaking, no facial drooping, no trouble walking (patient-reported negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Any weakness in an arm or leg, trouble speaking, or a droop in your face?
Are you steady on your feet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my arms and legs feel normal and strong, my speech feels fine, my face isn't drooping and I can walk okay.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No severe throbbing headache, no nausea, no photophobia, no visual aura of flashing lights
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a headache, feel sick, or notice lights bothering you?
Any flashing lights or zig-zag patterns?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No bad headache, I don't feel sick, lights aren't bothering me and there are no flashing lights.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is highly anxious and explicitly fears a stroke and multiple sclerosis
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling about all this?
What are you most worried it could be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really scared — I thought I was having a stroke, and now I'm worried this is the start of MS.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms came on gradually over an hour or two rather than reaching maximum severity instantly
trigger_question_examples: Did it come on all at once, or build up over time?
Was it at its worst straight away?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It built up over an hour or two — it wasn't there one second and full-blown the next.
